other_observers: Bill Kaempfer, Tom Wilberding, Doug Faulkner, Jim Schmoker, Eric Zorawowicz, Thomas Heinrich, Steve Larson, many others; Found by Ted Floyd
specific_location: SW corner of Boulder Reservoir near west entrance station.
description: Medium-sized shorebird with bright orange legs, oddly small-headed and pot-bellied look, and boldly patterned upperparts. Bird was overall about the size of a nearby Lesser Yellowlegs but more bulky. Bill all dark, moderately long and thin, slightly downturned. Head mostly buffy brown, a bit darker around the eye giving it a slightly masked look. Head disproportionately small looking compared to body. Legs fairly long, bright orange. Upperpart feathers mostly dark centered (nearly black), with wide buffy margins. Breast white, with uneven buffy and nearly black splotching on the upper parts but mostly white lower, transitioning to nearly all white on the undertail coverts. Primaries about even with end of tail. Tertiaries nearly black with wide bold buffy barringBehaviors: In my two observations the bird alternated between feeding and sleeping. It fed anywhere between the water's edge to belly-deep water. It associated with a Lesser Yellowlegs much of the time.Call: not heardPlumage: probably faeder-type male
photographer: Photographer: Bill Schmoker, Bill Schmoker, Bill Schmoker
